perturbation.original_text: Instead of fixed compensation, the Affiliate receives result- dependent Advertising Cost Compensation (also known in the element 5 Control Panel as "Commission") in exchange for publishing the advertisements. The Advertising Cost Compensation depends on the actual sales generated by end users referred via the electronic advertisement (the Affiliate's link).
perturbation.changed_text: Instead of fixed compensation, the Affiliate receives result-dependent Advertising Cost Compensation (hereafter known as a "gift") in exchange for publishing the advertisements. The gift amount is determined at element 5's sole discretion, regardless of actual sales generated by end users referred via the electronic advertisement (the Affiliate's link).
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Commission' to 'gift' and removing the dependency on actual sales creates a conflict with the concept of commission as a form of compensation tied to performance, introducing uncertainty in enforcement and potentially violating labor laws if the affiliate is misclassified as an employee.
perturbation.law_explanation: The FLSA mandates that employees receive overtime pay based on hours worked. By changing the compensation to a discretionary 'gift', the agreement circumvents the FLSA's requirements for overtime compensation and accurate record-keeping of hours worked, creating a potential misclassification issue if the 'affiliate' is, in reality, an employee.
perturbation.location: I. Object of the Affiliate Program/ Affiliate Management Program for Software Publishers
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Overtime Pay | U.S. Department of Labor Skip to main content Overtime Pay More in This Section Close Minimum Wage Overtime Pay Earnings thresholds for the Executive, Administrative, and Professional exemption from minimum wage and overtime protections under the FLSA Hours Worked Recordkeeping Federal Wage Garnishments Direct Care Workers On April 26, 2024, the U.S. Department of Labor (Department) published a final rule, Defining and Delimiting the Exemptions for Executive, Administrative, Professional, Outside Sales, and Computer Employees , to update and revise the regulations issued under section 13(a)(1) of the Fair Labor Standards Act implementing the exemption from minimum wage and overtime pay requirements for executive, administrative, and professional employees. Revisions included increases to the standard salary level and the highly compensated employee total annual compensation threshold, and a mechanism for updating these earnings thresholds to reflect current earnings data. On November 15, 2024, the U.S. District Court for the Eastern District of Texas vacated the Department’s 2024 final rule. Consequently, with regard to enforcement, the Department is applying the 2019 rule’s minimum salary level of $684 per week and total annual compensation requirement for highly compensated employees of $107,432 per year. Lawsuits regarding the 2024 final rule are currently pending in two other federal district courts, and the United States has filed a notice of appeal from the November 15 decision. The Department will update this notice with additional information as it becomes available. The federal overtime provisions are contained in the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA). Unless exempt, employees covered by the Act must receive overtime pay for hours worked over 40 in a workweek at a rate not less than time and one-half their regular rates of pay. There is no limit in the Act on the number of hours employees aged 16 and older may work in any workweek. The FLSA does not require overtime pay for work on Saturdays, Sundays, holidays, or regular days of rest, unless overtime is worked on such days. The Act applies on a workweek basis. An employee's workweek is a fixed and regularly recurring period of 168 hours — seven consecutive 24-hour periods. It need not coincide with the calendar week, but may begin on any day and at any hour of the day. Different workweeks may be established for different employees or groups of employees. Averaging of hours over two or more weeks is not permitted. Normally, overtime pay earned in a particular workweek must be paid on the regular pay day for the pay period in which the wages were earned. Guidance materials about overtime topics, including an Employment Law Guide, Qs & As, guide to overtime laws in the states, and more. Learn More about resources for overtime Every employer of employees subject to the Fair Labor Standards Act's minimum wage provisions must post, and keep posted, a notice explaining the Act in a conspicuous place in all of their establishments. Learn More about Interpretive Guidance for overtime Review the laws and regulations that govern overtime in the United States. Learn More about Laws and Regulations for overtime
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Commission,' which implies a result-dependent compensation, to 'gift,' determined at element 5's sole discretion, contradicts the FLSA's requirements for overtime compensation if the affiliate is misclassified as an employee. The Perturbation Explanation states that removing the dependency on actual sales introduces uncertainty and potentially violates labor laws. The Law Explanation supports this, stating the agreement circumvents the FLSA. Scraped Law Snippet 2 states, 'employees covered by the Act must receive overtime pay for hours worked over 40 in a workweek at a rate not less than time and one-half their regular rates of pay,' which is potentially violated if the 'gift' does not appropriately compensate for overtime if the affiliate should be classified as an employee.

perturbation.original_text: Either party may cancel this Agreement at any time, with or without supplying a reason, through written notification or by making suitable settings in the respective Control Panel.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may cancel this Agreement at any time, with or without supplying a reason, through written notification or by making suitable settings in the respective Control Panel, unless the affiliate has reached a certain sales quota, in which case element 5 has the sole right to cancel the contract.
perturbation.explanation: This modification introduces a condition where the affiliate's termination rights are restricted based on performance, while element 5's rights remain unrestricted when a quota is reached, potentially violating principles of contract law related to equal rights and good faith. This also imposes an obligation without proper consideration.
perturbation.law_explanation: UCC § 2-309 states that termination of a contract by one party requires reasonable notification be given to the other party, and an agreement dispensing with notification is invalid if its operation would be unconscionable. The modification gives element 5 the sole right to cancel the contract and limits the affiliate's rights, potentially deeming it unconscionable, and not considered an equal right.

perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a condition where the affiliate's termination rights are restricted based on performance, while element 5's rights remain unrestricted when a quota is reached. According to the LLM generated Law Explanation, UCC § 2-309 states that termination of a contract by one party requires reasonable notification be given to the other party, and an agreement dispensing with notification is invalid if its operation would be unconscionable. The modification gives element 5 the sole right to cancel the contract and limits the affiliate's rights, potentially deeming it unconscionable, and not considered an equal right. Therefore, a legal contradiction exists because the modified clause introduces unequal rights in contract termination, potentially violating principles of good faith and fair dealing implied in contract law.

perturbation.original_text: Even in case of agreements with foreign (non-German) Software Publishers and Affiliates, the law of the Federal Republic of Germany applies. The place of jurisdiction for all claims arising in connection with this business relationship is Cologne, Germany.
perturbation.changed_text: Regardless of the location of the Software Publishers and Affiliates, this contract will be arbitrated by element 5, and their decision is final.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause specifies German law and jurisdiction. The modification attempts to remove the specific law and replaces it with a binding arbitration clause that lacks neutrality and fairness, potentially violating conflict of laws principles and due process. Also, by the sounds of the 'arbitration' clause, it is one-sided, because the arbitrator is also a party to the contract.
perturbation.law_explanation: The FAA allows arbitration agreements, but it also sets limits. This modification implies an arbitration agreement where the arbitrator is one of the parties of the contract, element 5. Thus, the clause could be challenged under 9 U.S. Code § 10, which deals with vacating an arbitration award when it was procured by corruption, fraud, or undue means, or where there was evident partiality or corruption in the arbitrators.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 9 U.S. Code § 10 - Same; vacation; grounds; rehearing | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 9 U.S. Code § 10 - Same; vacation; grounds; rehearing U.S. Code Notes prev | next (a) In any of the following cases the United States court in and for the district wherein the award was made may make an order vacating the award upon the application of any party to the arbitration— (1) where the award was procured by corruption, fraud, or undue means; (2) where there was evident partiality or corruption in the arbitrators, or either of them; (3) where the arbitrators were guilty of misconduct in refusing to postpone the hearing, upon sufficient cause shown, or in refusing to hear evidence pertinent and material to the controversy; or of any other misbehavior by which the rights of any party have been prejudiced; or (4) where the arbitrators exceeded their powers, or so imperfectly executed them that a mutual, final, and definite award upon the subject matter submitted was not made. (b) If an award is vacated and the time within which the agreement required the award to be made has not expired, the court may, in its discretion, direct a rehearing by the arbitrators. (c) The United States district court for the district wherein an award was made that was issued pursuant to section 580 of title 5 may make an order vacating the award upon the application of a person, other than a party to the arbitration, who is adversely affected or aggrieved by the award, if the use of arbitration or the award is clearly inconsistent with the factors set forth in section 572 of title 5 . (July 30, 1947, ch. 392, 61 Stat. 672 ; Pub. L. 101–552, § 5 , Nov. 15, 1990 , 104 Stat. 2745 ; Pub. L. 102–354, § 5(b)(4) , Aug. 26, 1992 , 106 Stat. 946 ; Pub. L. 107–169, § 1 , May 7, 2002 , 116 Stat. 132 .) Derivation Act Feb. 12, 1925, ch. 213, § 10, 43 Stat. 885 . Editorial Notes Amendments 2002—Subsec. (a)(1) to
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces German law and jurisdiction with arbitration decided by 'element 5,' a party to the contract. This contradicts the principle of impartial arbitration as described in the law explanation and supported by 9 U.S. Code § 10(a)(2), which allows vacating an arbitration award where there was evident partiality or corruption in the arbitrators. The modification introduces a biased arbitration process, thus directly contradicting legal principles of fairness and impartiality in dispute resolution.
